title: MCP & Evaluation Capstone
concept: Combining MCP tool integration with a full RAG evaluation harness on one worked system; 'Next up: Production Capstone.'.

exercises.prompt: When a user receives a wrong answer from your RAG pipeline, how do you locate the bug?

options.text: You should rewrite the entire LLM prompt from scratch immediately.
You trace the pipeline logs backward from the bad answer to find where the error was introduced.
You delete the database index and rebuild it hoping the error disappears.

exercises.explain: Tracing a failure means working backward step-by-step from the bad response to find the exact point (like the retriever or the generator) where the error started.

cards.title: The Grand Finale
cards.body: We've learned how to trace errors step-by-step and connect external tools. Now, let's tie it all together by building a self-testing RAG pipeline that uses MCP tools and measures its own performance.

cards.title: MCP & Evaluation Capstone
cards.body: A capstone system brings everything together: Model Context Protocol (MCP) handles connecting external data sources, while an evaluation harness tests the system's accuracy automatically over dozens of scenarios.

cards.title: The Factory Assembly Line
cards.body: Think of an auto assembly line. Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools are the mechanical arms adding new parts, and the evaluation harness is the safety inspector checking every car for defects before it leaves the factory.

cards.title: A Complete System Test
cards.body: We connect a database tool via MCP to our system. Then, we run an automated evaluation script that feeds 50 customer questions to the system, calculating how many answers are factually correct based on the retrieved data.

cards.title: Evaluation is Never 'Done'
cards.body: You might think evaluating a system is a one-time task before launch. In reality, as your external data sources update via MCP, your evaluation harness must run continuously to catch new errors.

cards.title: Connecting vs. Evaluating
cards.body: Let's look at how data and quality control flow together in a unified system.

left.text: MCP tools fetch real-time context from databases and external APIs.

right.text: Evaluation harness scores the final outputs for accuracy.

exercises.prompt: How do MCP tools and an evaluation harness work together in a complete RAG system?

options.text: MCP generates the final answers, while the evaluation harness retrieves the data.
MCP is only used for training, while the evaluation harness runs in production.
MCP connects to the external data tools, while the evaluation harness measures response quality.
There is no connection; they are incompatible technologies.

exercises.explain: MCP acts as the standardized bridge to fetch the raw data, and the evaluation harness measures if the final LLM response is accurate and relevant based on that data.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Combining MCP tools with an evaluation harness makes it easier to swap data sources without breaking your tests.
exercises.explain: Using MCP allows you to swap or update databases without rewriting your evaluation tests, making the whole system modular and maintainable.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of automated testing in RAG systems:
exercises.sentence: To guarantee quality, developers run an evaluation harness to measure accuracy across test queries.
exercises.explain: An evaluation harness automatically tests your pipeline's outputs against target datasets, ensuring system reliability.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sounds like a report from an automated RAG evaluation harness using MCP?

options.text: I ran the python script twice and it looked pretty good both times, so the system is ready to launch.
Test run complete. MCP database tool successfully queried. Average faithfulness score: 0.92. Context recall: 0.88.
We asked a coworker to test the search bar and she said the database connection was fast.

exercises.explain: Option B sounds like a realistic AI-generated logs overview from a test run analyzing retrieval and faithfulness metrics over an MCP tool database hook.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a production-ready RAG system that uses MCP tools to query live product inventory. How should you set up your architecture to ensure both tool safety and high answer quality?

options.text: Only evaluate the LLM generator and ignore the MCP tool retrieval outputs.
Standardize tool access via MCP and run automated test queries to measure retrieval relevance and generation accuracy.
Hardcode custom database connectors and rely solely on user complaints to find bugs.
Disable MCP tools entirely to make the evaluation harness run faster.

exercises.explain: By standardizing tools with MCP and evaluating the entire pipeline programmatically, we can isolate failures to the retrieval stage (which lacks context) or the generation stage (which ignores context), ensuring high-quality outputs. Next up: Production Capstone.
